design_philosophy.core_loop: Spray Donkey Kong upward with bug spray while managing insects that steal flowers; complete level by forcing DK to top or eliminating all bugs
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (limited spray duration) vs. pest control (insects stealing flowers reduce end-of-level bonus) vs. boss pressure (DK descending)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Exterminator protagonist Stanley defending his greenhouse from a rampaging Donkey Kong and insect infestation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical departure from platformer expectations of series predecessors—shifts from jumping/climbing to vertical push-mechanics and shooting; replaces Mario with Stanley the exterminator; emphasizes spray-based combat over platforming skill, making it the 'black sheep' of the Donkey Kong trilogy

difficulty.lives_system: Extra life awarded at 50,000 points, then 'survival of the fittest' mode thereafter
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints; three fixed-sequence levels repeat. Failure restarts current level
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Super Sprayer power-up (spawns when DK sprayed past it on vines) provides temporary boost to spray effectiveness, but only available once per life; players must manage spray duration strategically

progression.upgrade_system: Super Sprayer temporary power-up increases spray velocity, pushing DK upward faster for limited duration
progression.unlock_mechanism: Three fixed levels cycle repeatedly; progression measured by score and survival rounds rather than unlocking new content
replayability_hooks: Score-based progression (extra life at 50k points shifts to 'survival of the fittest' mode)
Multiple insect types with different behaviors requiring tactical spray management
Flower collection mini-objective affects level bonuses
Arcade leaderboard culture (world record referenced at 3,538,000 points)

world_building_techniques: Setting established via greenhouse aesthetic with flowers as collectibles
Stanley's profession (exterminator) justifies spray-based mechanics naturally
Insects as thematic antagonists (bees, inchworms, beetles) reinforce greenhouse pest-control fantasy

boss_design.philosophy: Donkey Kong serves as persistent obstacle rather than discrete boss encounter; players must continually spray him to force upward progression, encouraging attrition-based strategy
notable_bosses: Donkey Kong (primary antagonist throughout all three levels; hangs from vines at screen center; descends/ascends based on spray pressure; no phase transitions documented)

faction_indicators: Stanley vs. Donkey Kong antagonism (exterminator vs. nature rampaging)
Flower preservation as moral element (lost flowers = reduced bonus)
danger_signals: Queen Beespies shatter into deadly pieces when destroyed, creating hazard escalation
Multiple insect types with 'attacker bees' suggesting coordinated threats
Insects actively steal flowers, creating dynamic pressure mechanic
environmental_storytelling: Greenhouse setting with flower collectibles establishes stakes visually
Vine structure with DK at center communicates vertical climb objective
Insect variety (Buzzbees, Kabutomushi beetles, butterflies) creates ecological atmosphere

sources_used: wikipedia - Donkey Kong 3 gameplay section, reception, legacy
